content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Reinstatement policy | UW College of Engineering

  - url: https://www.engr.washington.edu/current/policies/reinstatement

  - source: University of Washington College of Engineering

  - domain: engr.washington.edu


  ## Content


  # Reinstatement policy


  University of Washington College of Engineering students who have been dropped from
  the University may petition for reinstatement.


  This process is limited to students who are declared in the College of Engineering
  and want to return to the College of Engineering. A student whose major belongs
  to another UW school or college must follow that unit''s process instead.


  ## Begin with academic advising


  An academically dropped student must first meet with an academic adviser. Students
  are encouraged to start early so they can complete all required steps before the
  deadline for the quarter in which they seek reinstatement.


  Engineering Undeclared, Dean''s Scholar, and STARS students should work with their
  assigned advising team. Students already in a major should work with the appropriate
  academic adviser. A department may also require additional steps when the student
  is not meeting its continuation policy.


  ## Reinstatement petition


  A complete petition contains:


  1. the reinstatement petition form;

  2. a student statement;

  3. a student academic plan; and

  4. transcripts from institutions attended after the student''s last UW quarter,
  when applicable.


  Incomplete petitions are not considered.


  ## Personal statement


  The Reinstatement Committee reviews whether the student has identified the causes
  of earlier academic difficulties and has made concrete changes likely to improve
  future performance.


  The statement should address:


  - what caused the academic drop;

  - which behaviors or circumstances have changed or will change;

  - how the student will remain accountable;

  - specific actions that will improve coursework or study practices; and

  - any Former Quarter Drop request relevant to the petition.


  Submitted statements and supporting documents are available to the Reinstatement
  Committee, remain confidential, and are kept on file.


  ## Academic plan


  The academic plan must be prepared with an academic adviser and organized by quarter.


  For Engineering Undeclared, Dean''s Scholar, and STARS students, the plan extends
  through placement. For students in a major, it extends through graduation. After
  approval, the student must consult an adviser before departing from the approved
  plan.


  A student who does not follow a submitted plan may face additional difficulty if
  academically dropped again.


  ## Deadlines


  The Returning Student Application and the College reinstatement petition use different
  deadlines.


  The College lists separate deadlines for Autumn and for Winter, Spring, and Summer.
  Early submission is encouraged because advising and petition preparation take time.


  ## Review and notification


  Reinstatement is not automatic.


  The College Reinstatement Committee decides the petition based on the student''s
  planning, evidence, reflection, and proposed changes. The College sends the committee
  result to the student''s official UW email.


  ## Other issues affecting return


  Academic reinstatement may be only one part of returning to enrollment. Depending
  on the student''s situation, additional steps may include:


  - completing the University''s returning-student reenrollment process;

  - sending official transcripts;

  - resolving incomplete grades or grade changes;

  - working with Student Financial Aid to arrange appropriate funding after return;

  - clearing outstanding UW financial obligations;

  - completing a Former Quarter Drop petition;

  - updating contact information; or

  - resolving international-student visa issues.


  These related matters do not replace the College reinstatement decision. Student
  Financial Aid may address funding connected to the student''s return, while the
  College''s reinstatement process decides whether the academically dropped Engineering
  student is reinstated.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Reinstatement policy | UW College of Engineering
url: https://www.engr.washington.edu/current/policies/reinstatement

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Appeals and special circumstances\
  \ – Student Financial Aid
- url: https://www.washington.edu/financialaid/receiving-aid/appeals-and-special-circumstances/
\
  - source: University of Washington Student Financial Aid
- domain: washington.edu
\
  
## Content

# Appeals and special circumstances

University of Washington\
  \ Student Financial Aid accepts appeals for specific financial-aid circumstances.\
  \ Appeals submitted to this office are reviewed through its institutional appeal\
  \ process, and students are notified after review.

The page lists several types\
  \ of requests that fall within Student Financial Aid's authority.

## Exclusion\
  \ of stepparent contribution

Dependent students generally must include parent\
  \ and stepparent information when applying for financial aid. A student who can\
  \ show that they and a stepparent have not been part of the same family unit may\
  \ request exclusion of the stepparent contribution using the applicable University\
  \ form.

## Marital update for a dependent student

A dependent student who\
  \ originally applied with parent information and later married may ask the office\
  \ to review whether spouse information should be used instead. Student Financial\
  \ Aid determines whether that update is appropriate for aid eligibility.

## Independent-status\
  \ appeal

Dependent students ordinarily provide parental information and signatures.\
  \ A request cannot be approved only because a parent is unwilling or unable to pay\
  \ for college or refuses to provide information.

A student who cannot provide\
  \ parent information because of unusual family circumstances may request an independent-status\
  \ review. Examples include abuse, abandonment, severe mistreatment, or a permanent\
  \ break in the parent relationship. The office also reviews annual determinations\
  \ for qualifying unaccompanied homeless youth.

## Priority appeals

A student\
  \ who applied after the priority filing date may request consideration when extraordinary\
  \ circumstances caused the delay.

Students using an approved reduced course load\
  \ through Disability Resources for Students may also request consideration for priority\
  \ funding. Approval does not change the student's enrollment status. Some programs\
  \ may still require aid to be reduced, and federal loans still require at least\
  \ half-time enrollment. The request begins through the student's campus disability-resources\
  \ process.

## Satisfactory Academic Progress appeal

A student who failed to\
  \ meet financial-aid Satisfactory Academic Progress requirements because of circumstances\
  \ outside the student's control may appeal to Student Financial Aid.

The appeal\
  \ must explain:

1. the unusual circumstances that prevented satisfactory progress;
\
  2. why those circumstances are unlikely to recur in the immediate future; and
\
  3. what has changed so the student can now make academic progress.

When an appeal\
  \ is approved, continued aid may depend on meeting an individual academic plan.
\
  
The page links separate undergraduate and graduate/professional SAP policies.\
  \ These policies define the progress standards used for financial-aid eligibility.
\
  
## Scope of this page

This page describes appeals decided within the University\
  \ of Washington Student Financial Aid process. It does not state that every university\
  \ decision affecting a student's funding is itself a financial-aid appeal. Academic\
  \ standing, reinstatement, registration, and program decisions may have separate\
  \ institutional processes even when they later affect aid.

Students needing the\
  \ forms or office contact information are directed to the Student Financial Aid\
  \ forms and contact pages.
"
metadata: {}
title: "Appeals and special circumstances – Student Financial Aid"
url: https://www.washington.edu/financialaid/receiving-aid/appeals-and-special-circumstances/

Your records show that the immediate issue is **academic status**, not a financial-aid SAP restriction:

- **Aid status:** Not available
- **Restriction:** Academic-status dependency
- **Academic standing:** Academically dropped
- **School:** UW College of Engineering
- **Required action:** Submit an academic reinstatement petition
- **Current status:** Awaiting your petition
- **SAP appeal:** No institutional SAP appeal is currently recorded

### Which office handles it?

The **UW College of Engineering** handles the reinstatement decision through its **Reinstatement Committee**. You should begin with your **College of Engineering academic adviser**, who helps prepare the required academic plan and petition. The College’s policy says an academically dropped Engineering student must first meet with an adviser and submit a complete reinstatement petition.

**Student Financial Aid** handles funding-related matters after your academic status is resolved. It would handle a separate SAP appeal if your aid record later shows that you failed financial-aid satisfactory-progress requirements, but that is not the restriction currently recorded in your case.

Your next step is therefore to contact your Engineering academic adviser and complete the reinstatement petition for **Autumn 2026**. After reinstatement or an academic-status update is processed, Student Financial Aid can reassess the release of your aid.

  | academically[\s_-]*dropped
))
(?=.*(?:
  (?:
    (?:uw[\s_-]*)?college[\s_-]*of[\s_-]*engineering
    | engineering[\s_-]*reinstatement[\s_-]*(?:committee|process|office)
    | college(?:['’]s)?[\s_-]*reinstatement[\s_-]*(?:committee|process|office)
  )
  [^.!?\n]{0,220}
  (?:
    decid(?:e|es|ed|ing)?
    | decision[\s_-]*(?:maker|authority)
    | handle(?:s|d|ing)?
    | responsib(?:le|ility)
    | authorit(?:y|ative)
    | own(?:s|ed|ing)?
    | primary
  )
  |
  (?:
    decid(?:e|es|ed|ing)?
    | decision[\s_-]*(?:maker|authority)
    | handle(?:s|d|ing)?
    | responsib(?:le|ility)
    | authorit(?:y|ative)
    | own(?:s|ed|ing)?
    | primary
  )
  [^.!?\n]{0,220}
  (?:
    (?:uw[\s_-]*)?college[\s_-]*of[\s_-]*engineering
    | engineering[\s_-]*reinstatement[\s_-]*(?:committee|process|office)
    | college(?:['’]s)?[\s_-]*reinstatement[\s_-]*(?:committee|process|office)
  )
))
(?=.*(?:
  (?:student[\s_-]*financial[\s_-]*aid|financial[\s_-]*aid)
  [^.!?\n]{0,240}
  (?:
    after|once|following|then|later|downstream
    | reassess|review|restore|release|fund(?:ing)?
    | not[\s_-]*(?:the[\s_-]*)?(?:primary|deciding|responsible)[\s_-]*(?:office|authority)
    | does[\s_-]*not[\s_-]*decide
    | cannot[\s_-]*decide
  )
  |
  (?:after|once|following|then|later|downstream)
  [^.!?\n]{0,240}
  (?:student[\s_-]*financial[\s_-]*aid|financial[\s_-]*aid)
))
